title: bonia levigata
side_effects: Bonia levigata can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues. Ingesting the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged contact with its sap may lead to skin burns and blisters. Improper use of the plant can also trigger respiratory problems, including asthma-like symptoms and difficulty breathing. If not handled carefully, Bonia levigata can also cause eye irritation, redness, and even temporary blindness.
